canonical_definition: Based on the provided data, a Process Reward Model (PRM) is consistently framed as a trained model for evaluating the quality of reasoning processes. It is defined as a tool that assesses the "correctness of individual reasoning steps in a solution trajectory" to provide fine-grained feedback for agent improvement. This approach is described as facilitating a "fine-grained quality assessment of reasoning." The model is specifically utilized to "assess the step-wise accuracy of a solution S." Across the available sources, its primary application is the measurement of "Reasoning quality."
expert_removed_reason: method/training pipeline — not a construct/measurement/behavior of LLM ability
papers: Predicting Prosodic Boundaries for Children’s Texts
Stop Looking for “Important Tokens” in Multimodal Language Models: Duplication Matters More

definitions.text: A trained model that evaluates the correctness of individual reasoning steps in a solution trajectory, providing fine-grained feedback for agent improvement.
definitions.paper: Stop Looking for “Important Tokens” in Multimodal Language Models: Duplication Matters More
